items.text: NOTICE: Campus Wi-Fi maintenance is scheduled for Saturday, March 14, from 11:00 PM to 3:00 AM. Service will be unavailable across all academic buildings during this window. Students paying the $25 priority access fee may use the wired terminals in Whitfield Library throughout the outage.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will Wi-Fi service be unavailable?

options.A: From Friday evening until Saturday morning.
options.B: From Sunday noon until Sunday evening.
options.C: From Monday morning until Monday afternoon.
options.D: From Saturday night until early Sunday morning.

questions.explanation: The notice schedules maintenance from 11:00 PM Saturday to 3:00 AM, which spans Saturday night into early Sunday morning.

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the notice?

options.A: Wired access will also be unavailable during the outage.
options.B: All academic buildings will be closed during the maintenance.
options.C: Wi-Fi maintenance happens every weekend during the academic year.
options.D: Students who pay an extra fee can access the internet.

questions.explanation: The notice states that students paying the $25 priority access fee may still use wired terminals during the outage.

items.text: Spring is finally here! The Eastside Campus Community Garden is now open Tuesdays and Thursdays from 4:00 PM to 7:00 PM, and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM. Stop by anytime to volunteer, harvest fresh herbs, or just relax under the apple trees!

questions.stem: According to the post, when is the garden open on Saturdays?

options.A: From 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM in the morning.
options.B: From 4:00 PM to 7:00 PM in the afternoon.
options.C: From 11:00 PM to 3:00 AM overnight only.
options.D: From 9:00 AM to 7:00 PM all day long.

questions.explanation: The post specifies Saturdays 'from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM.'

questions.stem: The word 'harvest' in the post is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, students are invited to gather fresh herbs from the garden.

items.text: NOTICE: Unclaimed items currently held in the Anderson Hall Lost and Found will be discarded after May 30. Students must visit Room 112 between 10:00 AM and 4:00 PM on weekdays to retrieve belongings. A $5 service fee is required for each item claimed.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do to retrieve lost items?

options.A: They must contact the campus security office by phone.
options.B: They must visit Room 112 during weekday afternoons.
options.C: They must email the Anderson Hall front desk staff.
options.D: They must complete an online form before any pickup.

questions.explanation: The notice instructs students to 'visit Room 112 between 10:00 AM and 4:00 PM on weekdays.'

questions.stem: The word 'discarded' in the notice is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, items will be discarded — removed and disposed of — after the deadline.

items.text: NOTICE: Water fountains on the second floor of Stewart Hall will be closed for maintenance from April 8 through April 12. Bottled water is available from the second-floor vending machine for $1.50 per bottle. Working fountains remain available on the first floor.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the cost of bottled water from the vending machine?

options.A: One dollar for each bottle of water.
options.B: One dollar and fifty cents for each bottle.
options.C: Two dollars and fifty cents for each bottle.
options.D: Three dollars and twenty cents for each bottle.

questions.explanation: The notice states that bottled water is sold for $1.50 per bottle, or one dollar and fifty cents.

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the notice?

options.A: All campus water fountains will be closed during April.
options.B: Bottled water from the vending machine is free of charge.
options.C: Students can still use water fountains on the first floor.
options.D: The maintenance schedule will be extended through the summer.

questions.explanation: The notice explicitly says working fountains remain available on the first floor during the maintenance.
